title: Preserving Housing Wealth for Vulnerable Owners through Technology and Estate Planning
description: Homeownership is a major source of wealth for Black families, but without a will or estate plan, that wealth can get stuck in legal limbo through tangled titles and heirs' property issues. Financial technology is emerging as a tool to make estate planning more affordable and accessible for homeowners who want to pass property down to future generations.
summary: Homeownership is a major source of wealth for Black families, but without a will or estate plan, that wealth can get stuck in legal limbo through tangled titles and heirs' property issues. Financial technology is emerging as a tool to make estate planning more affordable and accessible for homeowners who want to pass property down to future generations.

title: Consumer Complaint Database
description: This article explains how the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's complaint database works, including when complaints are published and how they are used to regulate financial products and services. Consumers can use this database to research companies and make more informed financial decisions, though the data is not a statistically representative sample of all consumer experiences.
summary: This article explains how the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's complaint database works, including when complaints are published and how they are used to regulate financial products and services. Consumers can use this database to research companies and make more informed financial decisions, though the data is not a statistically representative sample of all consumer experiences.

title: Authority of the Board and Board Committees
description: This article explains how the Board of Directors at a government-sponsored mortgage enterprise operates under conservatorship, with the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) maintaining oversight and approval authority over key decisions. The Board must notify FHFA of significant changes to business operations, credit policies, and loss mitigation strategies before taking action.
summary: This article explains how the Board of Directors at a government-sponsored mortgage enterprise operates under conservatorship, with the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) maintaining oversight and approval authority over key decisions. The Board must notify FHFA of significant changes to business operations, credit policies, and loss mitigation strategies before taking action.

title: Equal Housing Opportunity
description: Community members share advice on finding the best neighborhoods to live in Houston, with recommendations including Montrose, Galleria, Heights, and Rice Village. Key tips include researching reviews before renting, prioritizing safety, and avoiding corporate landlords in favor of private owners.
summary: Community members share advice on finding the best neighborhoods to live in Houston, with recommendations including Montrose, Galleria, Heights, and Rice Village. title: The World Cup has arrived. Which real estate markets are cashing in?
description: The 2026 World Cup is creating notable opportunities in real estate markets across host cities like New York and Los Angeles, with short-term rental hosts seeing increased demand. However, questions remain about whether the economic benefits will meaningfully reach local property owners and businesses after accounting for public hosting costs.
summary: The 2026 World Cup is creating notable opportunities in real estate markets across host cities like New York and Los Angeles, with short-term rental hosts seeing increased demand. title: How Kanye’s former Malibu mansion showed the cracks in a home-flipper’s model
description: This article examines how a high-profile Malibu mansion — gutted by Kanye West and sold at a massive loss — exposed vulnerabilities in a home-flipper's financing model. It explores the risks of leveraged real estate deals and the challenges facing smaller developers trying to democratize property investment.
summary: This article examines how a high-profile Malibu mansion — gutted by Kanye West and sold at a massive loss — exposed vulnerabilities in a home-flipper's financing model. It explores the risks of leveraged real estate deals and the challenges facing smaller developers trying to democratize property investment.

title: 10 signs you're dating an emotionally unavailable man (and what to do) — Calm Blog
description: This article explores the signs of emotional unavailability in a romantic partner and offers practical steps for addressing the imbalance. It encourages readers to clarify their own needs, communicate directly, and evaluate whether the relationship is meeting those needs over time.

title: How to give advice: 7 tips to help you strike the right tone — Calm Blog
description: This article offers seven practical tips for giving advice in a way that feels supportive rather than critical, emphasizing active listening and empathetic communication. Small intentional shifts in how you frame and deliver guidance can make your support easier for others to hear and act on.

title: 14 thoughtful Father’s Day gifts for all types of dads — Calm Blog
description: This article offers 14 Father's Day gift ideas for various types of dads across a range of budgets, including free options like handwritten letters and framed photos. It focuses on meaningful, personal gestures that strengthen family bonds without requiring significant financial investment.

title: Best meditation music: 7 types to try in your practice — Calm Blog
description: This article explores seven types of meditation music, including ambient, instrumental, and 432 Hz tracks, to help beginners and experienced meditators find the right sound for their practice. It also addresses common questions about the best sounds for falling asleep and quieting a restless mind.

title: Feeling stuck when you meditate? These 9 meditation tips can help — Calm Blog
description: This article offers 9 practical tips for when meditation feels difficult, reassuring readers that distraction and restlessness are normal parts of the practice. It provides actionable techniques to help you refocus and build a more sustainable meditation habit without self-judgment.

title: Meditation for anger: 7 tips to feel calmer fast — Calm Blog
description: This article offers 7 practical meditation techniques specifically designed to help manage anger in everyday high-stress situations like tense meetings and long workdays. The tips focus on simple, accessible tools like breathwork and short pauses that can interrupt anger before it escalates.

title: How to use mindfulness meditation to ease anxiety disorders — Calm Blog
description: This article explains how mindfulness meditation can be tailored to specific anxiety disorders like generalized anxiety disorder and social anxiety disorder. It offers practical techniques such as thought labeling and redirecting attention to help manage persistent worry and self-monitoring behaviors.

title: What’s fueling AI companies’ IPO rush
description: Three major AI and tech companies — Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX — are all expected to go public in 2025, opening investment opportunities to everyday investors. However, going public comes with trade-offs, as companies may shift priorities toward shareholder profits in ways that could affect their products and values.

However, going public comes with trade-offs, as companies may shift priorities toward shareholder profits in ways that could affect their products and values.

title: How the Iran war could drive conflicts in countries thousands of miles away
description: The ongoing Iran conflict is causing ripple effects on global economies, driving up fuel and food prices in countries far removed from the conflict zone. Rising costs are triggering protests and political instability, with everyday people bearing the financial burden of disrupted supply chains and sanctions.
summary: The ongoing Iran conflict is causing ripple effects on global economies, driving up fuel and food prices in countries far removed from the conflict zone. Rising costs are triggering protests and political instability, with everyday people bearing the financial burden of disrupted supply chains and sanctions.

title: Here’s why Republicans just stood up to Trump
description: This article examines a rare instance of Senate Republicans pushing back against President Trump's attempt to create a discretionary $1.776 billion anti-weaponization fund. It analyzes how congressional budget processes and political maneuvering can serve as checks on executive power consolidation.
summary: This article examines a rare instance of Senate Republicans pushing back against President Trump's attempt to create a discretionary $1.776 billion anti-weaponization fund. It analyzes how congressional budget processes and political maneuvering can serve as checks on executive power consolidation.

title: New college grads are doing better than the vibes suggest
description: Despite negative perceptions about the value of a college degree in the age of AI, new college graduates are actually earning significantly more than high school graduates, with median salaries around $80,000 versus $47,000. While the job market has challenges, historical data suggests that finishing college still provides substantial long-term financial benefits.
summary: Despite negative perceptions about the value of a college degree in the age of AI, new college graduates are actually earning significantly more than high school graduates, with median salaries around $80,000 versus $47,000.
